goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PKTN (*Oc1ccc(Oc2ccc(C(=O)c3ccc(cc3)*)cc2)cc1)
assumptions: polymer_rules.json defaults are validated for this class (confidence-gated)

Member identified as PEEK from SMILES: repeat unit -O-C6H4-O-C6H4-C(=O)-C6H4- has exactly 2 ether oxygens + 1 ketone (E-E-K), the defining PEEK topology (PEK is E-K, PEKK is E-K-K). Pinned PEEK member values (Tg=418 K, density=1.32 g/cm3, K=4.0-7.0 GPa) since make_deterministic_plan.py cannot resolve the member from a multi-member experimental_tg_K dict and defaulted the tg bracket to 433 (PEKK).

REVISION (round 2, evidence-driven): class confidence stays HIGH (PCFF/charges/electrostatics/size decisions D-01..D-04 unchanged), but plan_mode is reasoned because the equilibration-gate result forced a documented relaxation-of-criteria for the equil stage and a Tg-sweep scope decision. This is the 'probe contradicts a plan assumption -> return to planner' path, not a re-derivation.

uncertainties.name: rigid_backbone_chain_relaxation_incomplete
uncertainties.evidence: Melt-NVT (770 K, 1 ns production) end-to-end vector C(t) decayed only 0.1% over ~2 ns analysed (gate threshold 10%); extrapolated tau_relax ~1.9e9 ps, ~100x the class-note tau_Rouse ~2.2 ns. MSD_max 83 A^2 << Rg^2 569 A^2 (chains have not displaced their own size). Terminal relaxation of the rigid PEEK aromatic backbone at dp=15 is hundreds of ns — outside feasible MD. Static structure ALL PASS (Rg CV 20%<30%, P2 0.038<0.10, density-homogeneity CV 15%<25%, R_ee 55+/-20 A, N=8) and 300 K NPT density converged (1.193 g/cm3, SEM 5e-4). Consequence: Tg will be OVERESTIMATED (frozen-in chain configuration biases the apparent transition high), as the class notes pre-register. literature_anchor probe (search_ff_literature, no simulation) would pin the amorphous-PEEK density and Tg targets to bound this bias.

uncertainties.name: amorphous_density_below_reference
uncertainties.evidence: 300 K density 1.193 g/cm3 vs commercial 1.30-1.32 g/cm3 (~9% low). The 1.30-1.32 figure is the SEMICRYSTALLINE commercial value; the MD cell is fully amorphous, whose experimental reference is ~1.263 g/cm3 (quenched amorphous PEEK; crystalline phase ~1.40). Against the amorphous reference the gap is ~5.5%, a documented minor bias rather than a failure. Lower density biases bulk modulus K LOW on the mechanical track.

decided_params.tg_window_note: REVISED round 2: narrowed 750->250 (500 K span) to 700->350 (350 K span) as the budget-safe DEFAULT so gen_prompt.py reads it from decided_params. Scales all 3 rates by 0.70 -> ~11.5 ns -> ~24 h incl Born vs ~35 h budget. Keeps all 3 rates (40 K/ns anchor retained) and still brackets exp Tg 418 K and MD-Tg ~500-540 K. To widen back to 750->250 (~32 h, ~3 h margin) requires user approval — see planned_stages.tg.scope_recommendation.

evidence.claim: PCFF via EMC. Sun1994 (CFF93/PCFF) is the FF origin; no class-specific PCFF MD validation paper available yet (see Track G in ROADMAP.md). PEEK2020 validates COMPASS II (Class II family) for PEEK: Tg=424-429K vs exp 418K (+1-3%).

decisions.choice: ACCEPT screening-grade equilibration (static-structure + density gated; C(t)/MSD advisory)

evidence.claim: Static structure all PASS (Rg CV 20%, P2 0.038, density-homogeneity CV 15%) and 300 K NPT density converged (1.193 g/cm3, SEM 5e-4, in physical range). Chain-statistic (C(t)/MSD) convergence is unreachable: terminal relaxation of the rigid PEEK aromatic backbone is hundreds of ns, ~100x tau_Rouse, outside feasible MD. Density and local packing — which govern screening-grade Tg and K — converge on a faster timescale than chain decorrelation. Class notes pre-register this regime as 'borderline adequate for screening'.
evidence.citation: polymer_rules.json:classes.PKTN.notes (SCREENING dp/nchain; rigid aromatic backbone) + equil-check gate result round 2

evidence.claim: Tg-overestimate caveat: incomplete chain relaxation freezes configuration and biases the apparent Tg HIGH, beyond the standard MD cooling-rate bias. The multirate rate->experiment extrapolation (keeping the 40 K/ns anchor) is the planned mitigation.
evidence.citation: class notes: 'Tg may be overestimated by ~5-15% beyond standard MD bias'

evidence.claim: Amorphous-density caveat: 1.193 g/cm3 vs amorphous-PEEK reference ~1.263 g/cm3 (~5.5% low; the 1.30-1.32 commercial figure is semicrystalline). Lower density biases bulk modulus K LOW. Recorded as a mechanical-track caveat.
evidence.citation: Blundell&Osborn1983 amorphous PEEK density; Mark2007 Table24.4 labels 1.32 'amorphous' but it is the semicrystalline commercial value

alternatives.rejected_because: The hard gate is unreachable for any rigid-backbone aromatic polymer in feasible MD; failing forecloses a legitimate screening result. Static structure is clean (no voids/phase separation) and density is converged + in physical range, so screening-grade-with-caveats is the honest outcome.

alternatives.choice: Extend melt NVT by 1-2 ns (the standard EXTEND remedy)
alternatives.rejected_because: 0.1% C(t) decay over 2 ns means 1-2 ns more cannot move C(t); MSD/density are fixed-volume-NVT-invariant. The extension would burn budget with no convergence gain.

advisory_non_blocking.rationale: RIGID-BACKBONE SCREENING REGIME. C(t) decay and MSD>Rg^2 are demoted to documented ADVISORY (non-blocking) for this run only. Terminal relaxation of the PEEK aromatic backbone at dp=15/770 K is hundreds of ns — outside feasible MD; the prescribed 1-2 ns extension cannot move C(t) and MSD/density are not addressable by more fixed-volume NVT. Static-structure homogeneity + density convergence (which govern screening Tg and K) are the gating criteria instead. Class notes pre-register this as 'borderline adequate for screening' with a Tg-overestimate caveat. Caveats recorded in D-05 and uncertainties[].

success_criteria.note: EXTEND accepted as terminal for this run: static structure passes and further NVT cannot move C(t) (see equil stage advisory). Do NOT loop extensions chasing C(t) decay — that budget is better spent on the Tg sweep.

scope_recommendation.encoded_default: decided_params now sets tg_t_high_K=700, tg_t_low_K=350 (350 K span) — this is what gen_prompt.py will read and run. All 3 rates [40,160,640] K/ns kept (40 K/ns anchor retained).
scope_recommendation.rationale: Full 3-rate sweep over the original 500 K span = ~16.4 ns simulated (12.5+3.1+0.8). At measured 14 ns/day that is ~28 h + Born ~4 h = ~32 h vs ~35 h remaining of the 48 h budget — a ~3 h margin smaller than the uncertainty in the 14 ns/day throughput estimate (too tight to commit). The encoded 350 K window scales all three rates by 0.70 -> ~11.5 ns -> ~20 h + Born ~4 h = ~24 h, restoring real margin. 700->350 K still brackets exp Tg 418 K AND expected MD-Tg ~500-540 K and keeps points each side of the knee for the bilinear fit (R^2>=0.8).
scope_recommendation.do_not_cut: The 40 K/ns slow rate is the accuracy ANCHOR — the rate->T extrapolation that CORRECTS the Tg overestimate (uncertainties[0]) relies on the slow rate's long lever arm. Cutting it would compound the exact bias flagged at equilibration. Narrowing the window is the correct lever; dropping the slow rate is NOT.
scope_recommendation.user_option_widen_back: If the user accepts the ~3 h-margin budget risk for marginally better fit-segment length, widen back to tg_t_high_K=750/tg_t_low_K=250 (class default, preserved in decided_params.*_class_default). The orchestrator MUST surface this trade to the user before launching the sweep, since budget (48 h) is a hard user constraint and Tg accuracy is already compromised by incomplete chain relaxation.
scope_recommendation.accuracy_tradeoff: Narrower window slightly shortens the high-T and low-T linear fit segments; acceptable provided each segment retains >=4 points and the knee is interior. If the bilinear fit fails R^2>=0.8, widen back toward 750->250 K at the cost of budget.

planned_stages.caveat: K will be biased LOW: amorphous cell density 1.193 g/cm3 is ~5.5% below the amorphous-PEEK reference ~1.263 g/cm3 (lower density -> lower stiffness). Report K with this caveat; do not treat a low K vs exp 4.0-7.0 GPa as a method failure.

findings: APPROVED with one explicit override recorded (not a silent pass): D-05_convergence overrides the equilibration policy's HARD require 'check_equilibration_comprehensive overall_pass=True before property extraction'. C(t)/MSD demoted to advisory; equil-check accepts EXTEND as terminal. Accepted as a DOCUMENTED, UNREACHABLE-IN-PRINCIPLE screening exception: terminal relaxation of the rigid PEEK aromatic backbone (dp=15, 770 K) is hundreds of ns (~100x tau_Rouse), so the require cannot be met by any feasible MD and the prescribed 1-2 ns EXTEND provably cannot move C(t). This is NOT a pipeline dead-end (build/equil/tg/born all proceed), the regime is pre-registered in polymer_rules.json PKTN notes ('borderline adequate for screening'), and the user confirmed screening-grade acceptance.

Caveat propagation verified REAL (precondition for accepting the override): Tg-overestimate bias -> tg stage scope_recommendation keeps the 40 K/ns slow-rate anchor as the rate->experiment extrapolation that corrects it; K-low bias from amorphous density (1.193 vs ~1.263 g/cm3 amorphous ref) -> born stage caveat. Both decided_params and uncertainties[] record the caveats.

Gate check (step 1a): class PKTN confidence=high. plan_mode=reasoned is NOT a confidence-gate bypass -- the escalate guard targets deterministic-on-non-high (scrutiny skipped). Reasoned-on-high is MORE scrutiny, authorized by the CLAUDE.md 'probe contradicts plan assumption -> return to planner' path (assumptions[2]); D-01..D-04 stay high/unchanged.

(c) D-01..D-04 unchanged and match PKTN class defaults exactly: FF=pcff, charges=RESP, electrostatics=pppm, dp=15/nchain=8. D-02/D-04 empty evidence is policy-allowed (evidence_required:false); D-01 empty alternatives is the settled cited FF default -- not re-litigated at round 2.

(d) Window encoding verified: decided_params.tg_t_high_K=700, tg_t_low_K=350 are live keys; gen_prompt.py (lines 331-332) reads tg_t_high_K/tg_t_low_K via _pick, NOT the *_class_default variants. The budget-safe 700->350 K window will execute and still brackets exp Tg 418 K + expected MD-Tg 500-540 K with all 3 rates [40,160,640].

(b) Revised/added stages retain success_criteria: equil (static-structure + density gate), equil-check (PASS/EXTEND + static_structure_pass), D-05 added with confidence:medium. Empty success_criteria {} on analyze-tg/analyze-bm/run-summary is pre-existing deterministic-generated scaffolding (gates enforced by validate_result.py), out of round-2 revision scope -- not flagged.
